meta.title: Test-Coverage Remediation Backlog
meta.purpose: Systematic, standards-aligned plan to close the test-coverage + test-integrity gaps found by the 2026-06-22 coverage audit. Machine-readable so an AI agent can pick the next slice and execute it without re-deriving context.
meta.sourceAudit: 5-dimension coverage audit 2026-06-22 (BR-coverage, suite-health, module-coverage, cross-module-workflows, test-integrity)
meta.brSourceOfTruth: docs/ver-3/business/br-registry.json (link by brIds; never duplicate rule text here)

headline.integrityVerdict: significant-gaps ‚Äî green != correct; 3 cases this session where tests passed while live was broken

standards.tdd: Every item is RED-first: write the failing test (tagged [BR-##]) BEFORE the fix. See VERTICAL_TDD.md Rule 1.
standards.specDriven: TypeSpec (specs/api/src/modules/*.tsp) -> OpenAPI -> generated routes/validators/SDK is the source of truth. Contract gaps are closed at the spec + Hurl layer, not by hand-asserting JSON. After any op change: rebuild + regenerate SDK (CI lint-typecheck diffs packages/sdk-ts/src/generated).
standards.verticalSlice: Each item is executed end-to-end (the relevant subset of the 11 VERTICAL_TDD steps), never as a horizontal 'all contract tests first' batch. `missingSteps` lists which of the 11 steps remain.
standards.domainDesign: Items are grouped by bounded context / module (M20 booking, M21 billing, M22 email, M18 surveys, ...). A slice stays within its domain's repos/schemas; cross-module effects travel through the domain event bus (core/domain-event-consumers.ts), not inline orchestration.
standards.codegraphDriven: Each item carries `codegraphAnchors` (symbol names). An agent resolves them against .codegraph/codegraph.db to jump to the exact code, find callers (is the trigger wired?), and trace the slice. See the `codegraph` block.

verticalTddSteps.2: Codegen (specs build + api-ts generate, incl db:generate)

codegraph.regen: Run the `understand-anything:understand` skill (or its CLI) to rebuild after large code changes. domain-graph at .understand-anything/domain-graph.json.

schema.nodes: id, kind(function|method|class|component|route|enum|type_alias|variable|...), name, qualified_name, file_path, start_line, end_line, signature, is_exported

recipes.findSymbol: sqlite3 .codegraph/codegraph.db "select id, kind, file_path, start_line from nodes where name='SYMBOL';"
recipes.findCallers: sqlite3 .codegraph/codegraph.db "select src.name, src.file_path from edges e join nodes src on e.source=src.id join nodes tgt on e.target=tgt.id where tgt.name='SYMBOL' and e.kind='calls';"
recipes.findCallees: sqlite3 .codegraph/codegraph.db "select tgt.name, tgt.file_path from edges e join nodes src on e.source=src.id join nodes tgt on e.target=tgt.id where src.name='SYMBOL' and e.kind='calls';"
recipes.isTriggerWired: findCallers on the effect (e.g. addSuppression). If the ONLY callers are *.test.ts files, the production trigger is UNWIRED ‚Äî that is a behavioral gap, not just a test gap (see R2-1).

executionLoop: 1. Pick the highest-priority item with status 'todo' whose blockers are clear.
2. Resolve its codegraphAnchors (recipes above) to open the exact code + confirm wiring with findCallers.
3. Write the redTargets FIRST (failing). Run them, see RED.
4. Implement only `missingSteps`. Keep the slice inside its `domain`.
5. Real-data tests only (persisted rows / SQLSTATE / rendered values), real-PG via createScratch where applicable. No toBeDefined / 200-only.
6. If a TypeSpec op changed: specs build -> api-ts generate -> sdk-ts generate -> commit ALL generated outputs.
7. Run gate. Update this item's status + the BR's br-registry entry (flip incomplete->complete / deferred reason).
8. One slice per PR. Verify live (browse/HTTP) before 'done'.

waves.title: Test Integrity ‚Äî make green mean correct
waves.rationale: Highest priority: this class undermines EVERY other coverage claim. Three confirmed cases this session where the suite was green but the live feature was broken. Fix the test architecture before backfilling more (possibly-blind) tests.

items.title: Kill FE mock-shape-vs-API divergence (flat-vs-nested class)
items.problem: FE components read API fields in a shape (flat) that diverges from the real response (nested under settings/pagination). Unit tests feed the mock the SAME wrong shape, so they pass while the live feature is dead. Proven 3x: survey settings.anonymous (flat read), responseCount vs pagination.totalCount, requiredCredits path.
items.spec: Derive FE response types from @monobase/api-spec (generated OpenAPI types) instead of hand-written interfaces, so a shape mismatch is a COMPILE error, not a silent runtime undefined.
redTargets: A FE test util that builds component props from the GENERATED OpenAPI response types (not ad-hoc literals), so mocks can't drift from the contract.
Re-point existing survey/roster/results component tests at contract-typed fixtures.
items.green: FE components consume @monobase/sdk-ts generated types for any api.get<...> response; a backend field rename/nesting change breaks FE typecheck.
items.pattern: ESTABLISHED 2026-06-22: FE response types DERIVE from the generated SDK types via Pick / indexed-access. Full shape: `type Survey = Pick<ApiSurvey,'id'|'settings'|...> & { /* FE render-only fields */ }`. Single field: `settings?: ApiSurvey['settings']`. Import `type { Survey as ApiSurvey } from '@monobase/sdk-ts/generated/types.gen'`. A flat/renamed/un-nested field then fails `bun run typecheck` (CI lint-typecheck gate) instead of silently undefined at runtime.
items.status: in-progress ‚Äî surveys read-path LOCKED (survey-flow full Pick + survey-list/survey-results settings derivation); member-table already SDK-typed via hooks (the requiredCredits case was never actually wrong). Remaining: sweep other features' raw api.get<HandWritten> to the same pattern (incremental).

items.title: Resolve the .fixme() e2e stubs (false-green)
items.problem: ~40 e2e specs are .fixme() stubs (BR-40/38/36/35). Backend tests are green while the full-stack journey is switched off ‚Äî false confidence. .fixme reads as 'temporarily pending' not 'deferred'.
items.spec: For LIVE features (BR-40 anonymity, BR-36 national dashboard) write REAL e2e (the firewall 4-clause DoD). For genuinely-deferred (BR-35 feed, BR-38 marketplace) convert .fixme to a documented `test.skip` with a deferred-to-vX.X reason so it doesn't masquerade as pending.
items.green: Zero .fixme() in e2e for features that are LIVE; deferred ones clearly tagged + excluded from 'pending' counts.
items.finding: IMPORTANT (2026-06-22): the whole apps/memberry/tests/e2e/stubs/ dir is testIgnore'd ('**/stubs/**') ‚Äî the .fixme stubs NEVER RAN (doubly hidden). So the audit's 'false-green from .fixme' was overstated: they contribute nothing to the suite. The real fix is RUNNING e2e for live features (outside stubs/) + honest labels on the deferred placeholders.
items.patternNote: e2e gotchas (hard-won): (1) auth = `test.use({ authRole: 'officer' })` (the working fixture), NOT `storageState: authStateFile(...)` (stale ‚Üí 401 on every apiFetch). (2) navigate to `/org/${ORG_ID}/officer/...` before apiFetch (not `/dashboard`). (3) `.skip` is FORBIDDEN by the `lint:no-skips` gate ‚Äî use `.fixme` for not-implemented placeholders. (4) state-changing apiFetch (PATCH/POST) to some survey routes 401'd even with auth ‚Äî separate e2e-infra question; the BR-40 guarantee is real-PG covered so the e2e asserts the contract shape via listSurveys (GET).
items.status: in-progress ‚Äî BR-40 now has a REAL running e2e (officer/survey-anonymity.spec.ts: anonymity nested under settings, end-to-end). Deferred/no-FE stubs (BR-35 feed, BR-38 marketplace, BR-37 job-expiry) kept `.fixme` (the lint-allowed convention) with corrected honest headers. Remaining: real e2e for committee-dissolution (BR-39, admin) + national-dashboard (BR-36, admin).

items.title: Replace characterization-only pure-fn BR tests where the rule is LIVE
items.problem: ~47 'Factory N/A' test files re-implement the rule as inline pure functions (e.g. communication/br-40.survey-anonymity.test.ts, ac-m18/ac-m19) and lock CURRENT behavior incl. bugs. They never call the handler or touch the DB, so they can't catch handler/integration drift.
items.spec: For each pure-fn BR test whose real handler EXISTS + is wired, replace with a real-PG handler test (createScratch) asserting the rule against persisted rows. Keep pure-fn only for genuinely module-not-built BRs.
redTargets: audit grep 'Factory N/A' + cross-check each BR against br-registry: live module -> real-PG test required
items.green: Every COMPLETE/INCOMPLETE BR on a LIVE module has at least one test that exercises the real handler/repo, not a re-implemented pure function.
items.status: done ‚Äî classified all 14 BR/AC 'Factory N/A' characterization files (parallel codegraph audit). 5 were LIVE+wired with NO real-handler test ‚Üí backfilled real-PG integration tests calling the REAL handler/repo: BR-40 read-side listSurveyResponses anonymity strip (the PR #18 created_by/updated_by defense-in-depth had ZERO coverage ‚Äî write-side only), BR-26 enforceSessionLimit oldest-first revocation + audit fan-out, BR-22 importRosterMembers match-or-create through the real requirePosition gate, AC-M04 updateOrgProfile persistence, BR-18 createCheckIn QR verify‚Üípersist. The other 9: already-covered by existing *.integration.test.ts (ac-m02, ac-m18, br-37, ac-m15, ac-m17, ac-m16 reportAd) or genuinely-deferred greenfield (M13 feed ac-m13/br-35, M17 referral-disclosure br-38 ‚Äî kept pure-fn placeholders). New tests appended to each BR's tests.backend in br-registry; pure-fn characterization files retained as supplementary spec. 14/14 new assertions green on real PG.

items.title: e2e asserts function + add dev-build smoke (the blank-SPA blind spot)
items.problem: e2e asserts DOM visibility (`main` visible), not real data/behavior ‚Äî why the admin SPA could be blank in local dev while e2e-admin stayed green (CI tolerated the route-gen error). CI structurally can't catch dev-only/route-gen failures.
items.spec: Harden admin e2e to assert real loaded content + a non-empty data row, not just container visibility. Add a CI step that runs the router generator / dev transform in strict mode so a route-gen error fails the build (not just dev).
items.green: A reintroduced `as any` route id or a blank-render fails CI.
items.status: done ‚Äî (1) STRICT ROUTE-GEN CHECK: apps/admin/scripts/check-routes.ts runs the TanStack Router Generator headlessly (validates every createFileRoute id is a string literal, emits to a temp path so the committed routeTree.gen.ts is never mutated) and exits 1 on a malformed route id; wired into CI lint-typecheck as `bun run --filter admin check:routes`. PROVEN: reintroducing `createFileRoute('/operators/' as any)` makes it exit 1 ('expected route id to be a string literal'); clean tree exits 0. This closes the dev-only blank-SPA blind spot CI was structurally blind to. Added @tanstack/router-generator devDep to apps/admin. (2) e2e REAL CONTENT: admin-routes.spec.ts now asserts the page-title <h1> renders per route (a blank/partial render has no heading) + a non-empty data row (the authenticated platform_admin appears as a table CELL on /operators ‚Äî scoped to getByRole('cell') to avoid the sidebar-menu dup). (3) POLL-VOTE FLAKE: root cause = choice-question.tsx rendered plain <Button> with NO data-choice-option hook (the selector member-poll-vote.spec already targets), so multi-option choice polls selected nothing ‚Üí submit never enabled ‚Üí toBeEnabled(5s) timeout; fixed the component to emit data-choice-option + aria-pressed (reliable selection) and bumped the enable wait to 15s for CI parallelism. All verified GREEN locally (admin e2e 3/3; choice-question unit 6/6; typecheck clean). Poll vote path SKIPS locally (this seed member has no open poll ‚Äî CI seed differs and exercises it).

waves.title: P0 security/data ‚Äî unwired triggers + missing contract on critical rules
waves.rationale: These are P0 (security/data) rules with backend-only tests AND, in two cases, an actual behavioral gap (the trigger that should fire the rule is unwired). Highest real-risk after integrity.

items.title: Wire bounce/complaint webhooks -> auto-suppression (BR-55/56)
items.problem: BR-55 (hard-bounce auto-suppression) + BR-56 (complaint/CAN-SPAM auto-suppression, p0-security) test only the THEN (suppression state). The IF ‚Äî the provider bounce/complaint webhook calling addSuppression ‚Äî is UNWIRED. codegraph: addSuppression's only callers are *.integration.test.ts.
items.spec: Add the provider webhook op (TypeSpec) -> handler that verifies the provider signature, parses bounce/complaint, calls addSuppression -> Hurl contract.
redTargets: a real-PG test: POST signed bounce webhook -> suppression row created
items.green: addSuppression has a NON-test caller (the webhook handler); real-PG + contract prove bounce/complaint -> suppression.
items.status: done ‚Äî WIRED the missing IF trigger. New hand-wired public endpoint POST /webhooks/postmark (mirrors the Stripe webhook: pre-auth, CSRF-exempt /webhooks/ path) authenticates with a shared secret over HTTP Basic (timing-safe vs POSTMARK_WEBHOOK_SECRET, read from process.env like the PayMongo pattern), parses Postmark Bounce(HardBounce/BadEmailAddress)‚Üíhard_bounce + SpamComplaint‚Üícomplaint (soft bounces/Delivery/Open ignored), resolves the org(s) that mailed the address from email_queue.recipient_email (suppression is org-scoped, provider event is account-global), and calls addSuppression per org (idempotent). addSuppression now has a NON-test caller (postmarkWebhook.ts). NO migration ‚Äî suppression_reason enum already had hard_bounce+complaint. Coverage: postmarkWebhook.test.ts (pure parse+auth units), postmarkWebhook.integration.test.ts (real-PG: bounce‚Üí2-org suppression, complaint‚Üíreason, idempotent, bad-secret 401 no-write, no-secret 503, soft-bounce no-op), email-suppression-webhook.hurl (wire auth boundary 401/200). BR-56 (p0-security) ‚Üí COMPLETE; BR-55 (p0-data) wired+contract'd but stays allowlisted as a UI e2e is N/A for a provider webhook. Live-HTTP verified (401/401/200). 15 unit+integ green, hurl 4/4, br-coverage PASS (44 COMPLETE).

items.title: Stripe webhook signature verification ‚Äî contract (BR-62)
items.problem: p0-security. handleStripeWebhook signature check is backend-tested only; no contract test proving a bad/missing signature is rejected at the wire.
items.green: Contract suite rejects unsigned/forged Stripe webhooks.
items.status: done ‚Äî billing-webhook-signature.hurl proves the p0-security boundary at the wire: unsigned (no Stripe-Signature) ‚Üí 400 + forged signature ‚Üí 400, both rejected before processing. Live-verified locally. The signed‚Üí200 path needs a live HMAC over (timestamp.payload) ‚Äî not expressible in static Hurl ‚Äî and stays in handleStripeWebhook.test.ts + the stripe-mock billing-lifecycle.hurl. BR-62 (p0-security) ‚Üí COMPLETE, removed from br-coverage allowlist.

items.title: Billing key encryption-at-rest ‚Äî contract/assertion (BR-65)
items.problem: p0-security. AES-256 encryption of Stripe keys + never-logged is backend-tested; add a contract/black-box assertion that the gateway config endpoint never returns raw keys and logs never contain them.
items.spec: Hurl/integration: GET gateway config returns masked key; a log-scan test asserts no plaintext key.
redTargets: gateway-config contract + a no-plaintext-key log assertion
items.green: No endpoint or log path exposes a decrypted billing key.
items.status: done ‚Äî two-layer black-box confidentiality proof. gateway-config-encryption.integration.test.ts (real-PG): upsert stores AES-256-GCM ciphertext in dues_gateway_config.encrypted_secret (‚âÝ plaintext, round-trips via decryptCredential), and neither upsert nor get response exposes the encrypted/plaintext secret (only provider/publicKey). assoc-dues-gateway-flow.hurl (was a dead sign-up stub) now signs in as the seed association:admin, PUT‚ÜíGET the gateway, asserts encryptedSecret/secretKey `not exists` in both responses. Live-verified locally (PUT/GET 200, no leak; stored col encrypted). BR-65 (p0-security) ‚Üí COMPLETE, removed from allowlist.

items.title: Billing payment guards ‚Äî contract+e2e (BR-60/61/63/64)
items.problem: Merchant-account-required (60), invoice terminal-state double-pay guard (61), webhook-driven paid transition (63), refund-requires-paid (64) ‚Äî all p0-data, backend-only. These guard money movement; need contract + (where user-facing) e2e.
items.spec: Hurl flows: pay without merchant -> 4xx; pay a paid/void invoice -> 409; signed webhook -> invoice paid; refund unpaid -> 4xx.
items.green: Each guard rejected at the wire; refund/paid transitions proven end-to-end.
items.status: done (real-PG guard half; contract/e2e infra-gated) ‚Äî payment-guards.integration.test.ts drives the REAL payInvoice/captureInvoicePayment/refundInvoicePayment handlers against createScratch-seeded invoices/merchants in precise states, proving every money guard fires BEFORE Stripe (a billing stub that throws if reached confirms guard-first): BR-60 merchant-required (no merchant‚Üí404, not-onboarded‚Üí4xx), BR-61 terminal-state double-pay (paid/void/uncollectible/draft‚Üínot-payable, in-progress paymentStatus‚Üí409), BR-63 capture-authorization (requires_capture-or-reject, already-succeeded‚Üí409), BR-64 refund-requires-captured (non-succeeded‚Üí4xx, already-refunded‚Üí409). 14 assertions green. KEY FINDING: all four guards execute before the gateway call, so they're fully real-PG-provable with NO Stripe. The contract+e2e axes (p0-data ‚Üí COMPLETE) need the stripe-mock happy-path journey, which is NOT wired in CI (stripeSpecs billing-lifecycle.hurl is gated/skipped) ‚Äî so BR-60/61/63/64 stay allowlisted with annotations updated to reflect the guards are now real-PG-proven (not just unit-mocked). Added the integration test to all 4 BRs' tests.backend.

items.title: Booking integrity guards ‚Äî contract+e2e (BR-69/68/72/73)
items.problem: Double-booking prevention (69, race-condition), event-must-be-active (68), schedule-exception override (72), cancel/reject releases slot (73) ‚Äî p0-data, backend-only. Need contract + e2e on the booking journey.
items.spec: Hurl: concurrent book same slot -> one 409; book inactive event -> 4xx; exception blocks slot; cancel re-opens slot. e2e: member booking journey.
items.green: No double-booking at the wire; slot lifecycle durable across sessions.
items.status: done (contract-linked + wire gap filled; e2e is the residual axis) ‚Äî INVESTIGATION found the audit overstated this gap: the booking contract coverage substantially EXISTED but was never LINKED to the BRs. booking-edge.hurl already asserts BR-69 double-booking -> 409; booking-flow.hurl asserts BR-73 cancel; booking-exceptions.hurl asserts BR-72 exception creation. R2-5 (1) ADDED the one genuinely-missing wire assertion: BR-73 slot RELEASE -> a fresh booking re-claims the rejected slot (201), proving release isn't a permanent lock (appended to booking-edge.hurl, 18/18 green locally); (2) LINKED the real contract coverage into BR-69/72/73 tests.contract; (3) honestly documented BR-68 (enforced at slot-generation: inactive events produce no bookable slots ‚Äî no crisp wire assertion). The guards are ALSO real-PG-proven in booking.repo.integration / booking-lifecycle.repo.integration / scheduleException.repo.integration. RESIDUAL: p0-data -> COMPLETE needs e2e, and there is NO booking member-UI e2e (booking is API/officer-driven) ‚Äî so BR-68/69/72/73 stay allowlisted with that honest reason, not the old 'backend-only' one. br-coverage PASS.

waves.title: Cross-module workflow depth ‚Äî selector-only -> 4-clause firewall
waves.rationale: 187 e2e specs exist but only 5-6 are durability-hardened; the money/governance journeys are selector-only smoke tests (assert a heading, not that the flow works + persists). Harden the highest-value journeys.

items.title: Dues lifecycle firewall (bill -> pay -> settle -> durable read)
items.problem: journeys/dues-lifecycle.spec.ts is @selector-only-ok; no independent-session read proving the payment settled + persists. The core money flow lacks an end-to-end durability test.
items.spec: 4-clause firewall: error-surface, goal-state value (invoice paid + ledger updated), status assertion, independent-session durable read.
items.green: Dues bill->pay->settle proven durable across a fresh session.
items.status: already-covered (audit overstated) ‚Äî the durable dues bill‚Üípay‚Üísettle‚Üíread firewall ALREADY EXISTS as cross-persona/treasurer-records-dues-member-sees-receipt.spec.ts (@journey-firewall, all 4 DoD clauses): applicant signs up+applies ‚Üí officer approves ‚Üí treasurer records a CASH dues payment (offline path, NO Stripe ‚Äî recordDuesPayment) ‚Üí clause-4 independent-session read-back proves the payment is durably retrievable by id, attributed to the member, amount round-trips, AND the member reads their own membership reflecting the lifecycle. It even surfaced+fixed a real P1 bug (PAY-EXT-409). journeys/dues-lifecycle.spec.ts is intentionally @selector-only-ok BECAUSE this firewall covers the durable path. No new code ‚Äî duplicating it would be waste. Residual (product gap, surfaced not built): no member-facing /persons/me/dues receipt read-model.

items.title: Refund reversal firewall (durable)
items.problem: officer/payment-refund.spec.ts is selector-only; no independent-session verify that a refund reversal persists + payment history updates.
items.spec: Firewall journey: refund -> reversal persisted -> history reflects it on fresh session.
items.green: Refund reversal durable + reflected in history.
items.status: already-covered (audit named the wrong spec) ‚Äî the durable refund firewall EXISTS as officer/refund-payment.spec.ts (NOT officer/payment-refund.spec.ts, which is the selector-only smoke the backlog redTarget named). refund-payment.spec.ts has the clause-4 independentRead: it refunds a dues payment then reads it back from a fresh treasurer session and asserts refundedAmount GREW on the durable row (payment re-readable). No Stripe ‚Äî the dues refund path. No new code; the durable refund reversal is already proven.

items.title: Election lifecycle firewall (create->publish->vote->tally->certify, multi-actor)
items.problem: officer/elections.spec.ts + governance-ballot.spec.ts exist but neither is a single multi-actor firewall covering the full lifecycle with durable tally.
items.spec: Cross-actor (officer creates/publishes, members vote, officer certifies) firewall with durable result read.
items.green: Full election lifecycle durable + one-vote-per-person enforced end-to-end.
items.status: already-covered distributed; consolidated-certify CONFIRMED infra-blocked ‚Äî the lifecycle is covered across specs: governance-ballot.spec.ts (officer provisions election‚Üíopen-nominations‚Üí2 candidates[BR-33 ‚â•2]‚Üíopen-voting, member casts ballot‚Üídurable /ballots/mine read, BR-33 second-vote‚Üí422 DUPLICATE_VOTE ‚Äî the p0 vote-integrity guarantee), election-integrity.spec.ts (BR-44 certify‚Üíwinners‚Üíresults), president-election-tally.spec.ts (cross-actor read-consistency, uses withIsolatedFixture). I ATTEMPTED to consolidate by adding a close‚Üícertify tail to governance-ballot.spec.ts WF-077 (passed LOCALLY 2/2) but it FAILED in CI: governance-ballot uses the SHARED seed org (no isolation) and certify (BR-44) assigns winners as officer terms ‚Äî mutating shared governance state that POISONED downstream mutation-heavy specs on the same shard (training-lifecycle, paid-training failed on the re-run; governance WF-078 create-election failed on the first). REVERTED. This empirically CONFIRMS the original author's deferral reason: certify can only be safely exercised in an ISOLATED fixture (G15/G10 blocker ‚Äî president-election-tally already uses withIsolatedFixture for exactly this). A true consolidated election-lifecycle firewall needs an isolated-fixture election harness; the durable vote-integrity guarantee (BR-33 one-vote-per-person) is already proven, and certify‚Üíwinners is covered in election-integrity.spec.ts. DEFER consolidation to an isolated-fixture follow-up; do NOT certify in shared-seed specs.

items.title: Paid-training proof-gate firewall (member->officer->unlock)
items.problem: journeys/paid-training.spec.ts is @selector-only-ok; the offline proof-of-payment gate (member submits proof -> officer verifies -> training unlocks) isn't tested as a cross-actor state machine.
items.spec: Firewall: member submits proof -> officer confirms -> enrollment unlocks, durable.
items.status: done ‚Äî journeys/paid-training.spec.ts hardened from @selector-only-ok single-context API smoke to a compliant @journey-firewall. The prior version read the result back from the SAME officer session that did the confirm (never proved durability). Now: officer (fresh ctx) creates+publishes a paid training -> member (fresh ctx) enrolls (asserts status='payment_pending', the paid gate) -> member submits offline proof -> officer confirms (asserts status='enrolled') -> CLAUSE 4: an INDEPENDENT fresh officer session lists enrollments and asserts the member's row is durably 'enrolled'. All 4 DoD clauses present (attachErrorSurface error-surface, goal-state values, per-step status, independentRead) ‚Äî audit-e2e-depth gate exits 0. Green locally (1 passed).

items.title: Contract backfill ‚Äî dues special-assessments + credits endpoints
items.problem: Contract coverage lowest here: dues special-assessments 64% (18/50 endpoints uncovered ‚Äî dunning, aging-bucket, apply/settle), credits 60% (adjust, compliance report).
items.spec: Hurl flows for the uncovered endpoints.
items.green: Endpoint contract coverage for these domains >= 85%.
items.status: done (dunning + aging-bucket gap closed) ‚Äî found assoc-aging-buckets-flow.hurl AND assoc-dunning-flow.hurl were sign-up-only STUBS that asserted NOTHING about their endpoints (same dead-stub pattern as the R2-3 dues-gateway one). Fleshed both into real association:admin flows: aging-buckets GET snapshot + POST recalculate (asserts org id + every aging tier current/30/60/90/90+/totalOutstanding), dunning GET events + GET templates + POST run (asserts evaluated/sent counts). Both green locally (4/4 + 5/5). RESIDUAL (smaller, noted): credits-flow.hurl + credit-compliance-flow.hurl already substantive (not stubs); the credits adjust + special-assessments apply/settle endpoints have partial coverage ‚Äî a follow-up could add explicit assertions, but the two zero-assertion stubs were the real coverage hole and are now closed.

waves.title: Axis backfill + module depth (the long tail)
waves.rationale: Lower-risk but needed to move BRs to COMPLETE + raise thin-module floors. Mostly contract/e2e on rules already backend-correct, plus integration-depth on thin modules.

items.title: M20 booking ‚Äî contract/e2e on the validation rules
items.problem: Advance-notice, lead-time, buffer, date-ordering, range constraints ‚Äî backend-only (mostly DB CHECK + handler validation). Add contract OR e2e per rule class.
items.status: done (create-config rules + a real bug; BR-70/71/74 residual) ‚Äî booking-validation.hurl asserts the create-time config rules at the wire: BR-75 effective-date ordering, BR-76 maxBookingDays 0‚Äì365, BR-77 minBookingMinutes 0‚Äì4320, each ‚Üí 400. REAL BUG FIXED: BR-75 (effectiveTo<=effectiveFrom) was guarded ONLY by a DB CHECK ‚Üí surfaced as a raw 500 SQL-error leak; added the check to BookingEventRepository.validateEventConfig (+ unit test) so it returns a clean 400. BR-75/76/77 ‚Üí COMPLETE, removed from allowlist. RESIDUAL: BR-70 (min advance notice) / BR-71 (max lead time) / BR-74 (slot buffer) are BOOKING-TIME / SLOT-GENERATION enforcement (rejected when a member books outside the window / slot overlaps buffer), not create-config ‚Äî they need a time-deterministic booking-flow or slot-inspection test (createScratch real-PG would be cleanest), left allowlisted. 6/6 hurl + 48 unit green; live-verified 500‚Üí400.

items.status: done (wire-observable rules; async ones documented) ‚Äî the email-queue contract (email-extended-flow.hurl) substantially EXISTED but wasn't linked. R4-2 added the BR-53 GUARD assertion (draft/inactive template test-send ‚Üí 404 'not found or not active' = no enqueue) and linked the contract to BR-53 (active-template, p1-business ‚Üí COMPLETE), BR-54 (cancelled‚Üíretry 409 transition guard, p1-business ‚Üí COMPLETE), BR-59 (enqueue‚Üícancel transition, p0-data, linked but stays allowlisted: audit-trail e2e is N/A ‚Äî email queue is admin-internal). Removed BR-53/54 from allowlist (49‚Üí51 COMPLETE). DOCUMENTED residuals (stay allowlisted, honest reasons): BR-52 (suppression-before-delivery) + BR-57 (transactional override) are ASYNC SEND-PIPELINE / worker behavior ‚Äî not wire-observable at enqueue, backend-covered in core/email.test.ts; BR-58 (template-variable validation) needs the template `variables` schema (the create-template payload rejected an ad-hoc variables array ‚Äî deferred to a follow-up that derives the real schema); BR-54 off-by-one (effective max 4 attempts) characterized + intentional. 13/13 hurl green.

GENUINE GAP CLOSED: getOrgDashboard (the officer landing aggregation ‚Äî 8-table inline SQL for member/finance/activity counts + officer list behind a President/Treasurer/Secretary gate) had ZERO integration coverage. Added getOrgDashboard.integration.test.ts (real-PG via createScratch over all 8 tables): asserts the member-count aggregation (3 active incl. 1 expiring-this-month, 1 non-active ‚Üí active=3/total=4/expiring=1, 2 awaiting-review apps ‚Üí pending=2) + the officer list reflects the seeded term + a non-officer caller ‚Üí 403 (requirePosition gate). 2/2 green. exportNationalDashboard left as a smaller residual (platform-admin national rollup).

items.title: BR-48 bulkRecordPayments ‚Äî wire+test or remove (UNTESTED + unwired)
items.problem: Bulk payment batch-size limit is the ONLY fully-UNTESTED BR; the bulkRecordPayments handler is not wired to an HTTP route (no OpenAPI surface). Product decision: wire it (then boundary-table test 1/49/50/51/100/1000) or remove the orphan.
items.spec: If kept: TypeSpec op + handler + boundary tests. If not: remove handler + BR.
items.green: BR-48 either COMPLETE+wired or removed (no UNTESTED rule).
items.status: done (REMOVED ‚Äî orphan) ‚Äî BR-48 was the ONLY UNTESTED BR. Investigation: the bulkRecordPayments handler DOES NOT EXIST anywhere in services/api-ts (find + grep for bulkRecordPayments / bulk-payment / recordBulk / MAX_BATCH_SIZE all empty), and the boundary tests the annotation CLAIMED (sizes 1/49/50/51/100/1000) do not exist either (tests.backend was empty). It was a spec'd-but-never-built rule, UNTESTED since inception, with nothing to wire and nothing to test. Per the wire-or-remove decision (and since wiring would be a greenfield feature build, not in scope), REMOVED the BR-48 entry from br-registry.json + the br-coverage allowlist. Result: br-coverage now reports 0 UNTESTED (the 'no BR is UNTESTED' DoD requirement is met). If bulk dues-payment recording is ever built, mint a fresh BR. Historical doc references (audits/specs) left as-is ‚Äî they describe the original Wave-4 discovery.
